message.content:
{
  "round": 9,
  "parent_reward_id": "round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned",
  "selected_candidate_id": "round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned",
  "mutation_family": "progress_conditioned",
  "observed_outcome": {
    "coarse_result": "partial_progress_available_but_early_failure_dominant",
    "main_failure_remaining": "approach_progress still over-rewards early_failure trajectories; terminal_reward still misassigns positive reward to unsafe landings",
    "main_success_signal": "partial_progress trajectories remain abundant with distance improvement; proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout continue to favor positives"
  },
  "failure_repair_outcome": {
    "failure_before": "approach_progress over-rewarded early_failure; terminal_reward misassigned positive to unsafe landings",
    "repair_attempt": "Continued progress-conditioned mutation with heavy penalization of failures (failure avoidance score 0.9546, mean reward on failure -49.35)",
    "outcome_after": "Early_failure still dominant in trajectory judgment (27/50 vs 20 partial_progress), no success_like trajectories; structural deficiencies remain unaddressed"
  },
  "lesson": "Progress-conditioned refinements alone are insufficient to overcome structural component deficiencies. Component recomposition is required: restructure approach_progress to penalize early_failure and replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus.",
  "future_guidance": [
    "restructure approach_progress to strongly penalize early_failure trajectories (e.g., negative baseline or quality-based scaling)",
    "replace terminal_reward with crash_penalty (sufficiently negative) for unsafe terminations and safe_landing_bonus (positive under stringent conditions)",
    "preserve proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout",
    "preserve progress-conditioned gating (far/near stages)",
    "do not construct success_like preference pairs; no high-confidence success_like trajectories exist",
    "do not treat partial_progress as success_like"
  ],
  "archive_update": {
    "add_to_elite_archive": true,
    "rollback_triggered": false,
    "next_parent_reward_id": "round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned",
    "reason": "Selected candidate added to elite archive due to high failure avoidance score; serves as next parent despite structural issues"
  },
  "uncertainties": [
    "No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; preference construction forbidden",
    "Label inconsistency: coverage reports 33 success_like, trajectory judgment summary shows 0 in 50 samples",
    "Training return spike (760) in round 6 is likely an artifact, not genuine success",
    "Component_summary includes components not in parent code, making diagnosis noisy"
  ]
}
message.reasoning_content:
We are given input artifacts for round 9. We need to produce a reflection/memory record in JSON format. The input includes:
- round_index: 9
- analyzer_self_evaluation with detailed analysis
- selection_report: selected round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned
- coverage_report: coverage_type partial_or_success_only, decision_level no_decision
- trajectory_judgment_summary_optional: 50 trajectories, 20 partial_progress, 27 early_failure, 3 ambiguous
- tage_summary_optional: three candidates with reports
- rollback_report: no rollback, next_parent_reward_id = round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned
- elite_archive_optional: list of elites including the selected candidate (round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned) with score 0.675

Key points from analyzer_self_evaluation:
- Parent reward: round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned? Wait, the analyzer says parent_reward_id: "round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned". Actually in the input it's "round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned". But the rollback_report says next_parent_reward_id is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. So the selected candidate is the parent for next round.
- Failure summary: approach_progress over-rewards early_failure; terminal_reward misassigns positive reward to unsafe landings.
- Memory coverage: no_decision, no high-confidence success_like trajectories, label inconsistency (33 success_like in coverage, 0 in judgment summary).
- Component diagnosis: restructure approach_progress, remove_or_gate terminal_reward, keep others.
- Mutation intent: component_recomposition family with progress_conditioned secondary.
- Preserve components: proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout, progress-conditioned gating.
- Remove/gate: terminal_reward.
- Required changes: restructure approach_progress, replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus.
- Rollback not recommended.

Selection report: selected candidate round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned (progress_conditioned family) with static/novelty prior. Not component_recomposition. Why? Because candidate0 was component_recomposition but not selected due to lower static/novelty? Actually scores: candidate0 tage 0.5958, static 0.8; candidate1 tage 0.5778, static 0.9; candidate2 tage 0.5979, static 0.7. So candidate1 had highest static (0.9) and novelty 0.00155 vs others 0.00033. So selected mainly on static prior.

Coverage report: 928 trajectories, 822 partial_progress, 64 early_failure, 33 success_like, 9 low_progress_survival. Label margin sufficient, but decision_level no_decision. Cannot build preference pairs.

Trajectory judgment summary: 50 samples, 20 partial_progress, 27 early_failure, 3 ambiguous, 0 success_like. This confirms label inconsistency.

Tage summaries: all candidates have preference_consistency 0.0, failure_avoidance scores high (0.99 for candidate0 and candidate2, 0.95 for candidate1). candidate1 has mean reward on failure -49.35 (very negative), which might indicate it penalizes failures heavily. But note: candidate1 is progress_conditioned family, and it has crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus? Actually from component_diagnosis, candidate1 might be a progress-conditioned variant without those new components. But we need to infer mutation_family from the selected candidate: it's progress_conditioned. The analyzer's mutation_intent was for component_recomposition, but the selected candidate is not that. So we must reflect what actually happened.

Rollback: not triggered, next_parent_reward_id is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned.

Elite archive: includes round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned with score 0.675. The best is round0_candidate2 with 0.9. But note the selected candidate is now added to elite.

Now we need to fill the JSON output.

We should derive observed_outcome: coarse_result? Based on coverage, still partial_progress available but no success_like. Trajectory judgment shows many early_failure (27 vs 20 partial_progress). So main failure remains early_failure and terminal instability. main_success_signal: partial_progress distance improvement? But from analyzer, no success_like.

Failure_repair_outcome: The repair attempt in this round was a progress_conditioned mutation (selected candidate). But what did it attempt to fix? The candidate is from the same family, likely minor adjustments. But the analyzer had recommended component_recomposition. So the actual repair was a continuation of progress-conditioned fine-tuning. We can say the repair attempt was "continued progress-conditioned gating with emphasis on penalizing failures heavily" (since failure avoidance score 0.95, mean reward on failure -49.35). Outcome after: still high early_failure count in judgment summary (27/50), partial_progress 20, ambiguous 3. So outcome: early_failure not sufficiently reduced, no success_like.

Lesson: Progress-conditioned refinements alone cannot overcome structural component deficiencies; early_failure remains high and no success_like emerges.

Future guidance: based on analyzer diagnosis, preserve proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout; restructure approach_progress and replace terminal_reward.

Archive_update: add_to_elite_archive: true (selected candidate score 0.675, already in elite). rollback_triggered: false. next_parent_reward_id: round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. reason: selected candidate added to elite due to score > 0.6? Actually it's already in elite list. But we can state it becomes the parent for next round.

Uncertainties: list uncertainties from analyzer: no high-confidence success_like, label inconsistency, training return spike artifact, component_summary noise.

Now let's construct the JSON.

Note: parent_reward_id should be the parent of the selected candidate. The selected candidate is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. Its parent is the previous parent reward: from analyzer, parent_reward_id is "round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned"? But careful: the selected candidate is generated from some parent. In the selection report, it says candidate1, and its parent might be the one used to generate it. From the rollback_report, next_parent_reward_id is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned, meaning that will be the parent for next round. But for this round, the parent of the selected candidate should be the reward that was used to generate it. That is not explicitly given. But we have the analyzer's parent_reward_id: round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned (the reward being analyzed). That is the current parent before generation? Actually the analyzer's parent_reward_id is the reward being evaluated as parent. It says "parent_reward_id": "round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned". That might be the reward that was used as basis for generating candidates in this round. So the selected candidate's parent is that. But also in the selection report, it says the candidate is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. And in rollback, next_parent_reward_id is round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. So the parent_reward_id for this round's memory is round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned. We'll set that.

selected_candidate_id: round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned.

mutation_family: For the selected candidate, from selection report: "mutation_family": "progress_conditioned". So mutation_family is "progress_conditioned".

observed_outcome: coarse_result: Based on coverage and judgment, still partial progress available but early failure is main. No success-like. So coarse_result: "partial_progress_available" or "early_failure_dominant". The analyzer says main_failure_remaining: over-reward of early_failure and misassignment of terminal_reward. So main_failure_remaining: "early_failure over-rewarded, terminal_reward misassigns positive to unsafe landings". main_success_signal: "partial_progress trajectories abundant with distance improvement". We'll combine.

failure_repair_outcome: failure_before: "approach_progress over-rewards early_failure, terminal_reward misassigns positive to unsafe landings". repair_attempt: "Continued progress-conditioned mutation with heavy penalization of failures (failure avoidance score 0.95, mean reward on failure -49.35)". outcome_after: "Early_failure still dominant in trajectory judgment (27/50 vs 20 partial_progress), no success_like trajectories; structural component deficiencies remain".

lesson: "Progress-conditioned refinements alone are insufficient to overcome structural component deficiencies. Component recomposition is required: restructure approach_progress to penalize early_failure and replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus."

future_guidance: list from analyzer required_changes and preserve_components. Also include forbidden: do not treat partial_progress as success_like, do not construct success_like preference pairs.

archive_update: add_to_elite_archive: true (since selected candidate was added to elite archive as shown). rollback_triggered: false. next_parent_reward_id: round9_candidate1_progress_conditioned. reason: "Selected candidate became the next parent for continued evolution despite structural issues; added to elite archive for its failure avoidance properties."

uncertainties: list from analyzer: "No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; preference construction forbidden", "Label inconsistency: coverage reports 33 success_like, trajectory judgment summary shows 0 in 50 samples", "Training return spike (760) in round 6 is likely an artifact, not genuine success", "Component_summary includes components not in parent code, making diagnosis noisy".

We'll structure accordingly.

Make sure to output only valid JSON.
